title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will take place from July 11-16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries.
Previous Sail Boston events occurred in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, drawing millions of spectators.
The 1992 and 2000 events faced protests due to historical associations with the transportation of slaves.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots minicamp amid improved team morale following offseason controversies.
Reports indicate Vrabel maintains high energy, suggesting off-field issues haven't affected his coaching performance.
Newly acquired safety Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's consistent approach ahead of 2026's challenging schedule.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a game-winning 426-foot home run in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two earned runs, as the Red Sox defeated the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman earned the save, overcoming a bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning, securing the Red Sox win.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day IL by Red Sox with a sports hernia; Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester.
Story has struggled this season, batting .206 with six errors at shortstop; his injury dates back to spring training.
Red Sox considering surgery for Story's hernia, which could sideline him 6-10 weeks; Andruw Monasterio to replace him at shortstop.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: David Peloski, 74, was killed after his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
Thirteen students were on the bus; none were seriously injured. The bus driver sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
The crash occurred on Route 12; New Hampshire State Police are investigating with Marlborough police and fire officials.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th gives Braves a 3-2 win over Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs in five innings with six strikeouts.
Boston struggles offensively, leaving seven on base and hitting 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs's return to the Patriots is unlikely due to a pending A.J. Brown trade.
Diggs expressed interest in re-signing, but salary cap concerns hinder potential reunion with Patriots.
Patriots' offensive capabilities enhance with trade for A.J. Brown and receiver Romeo Doubs contract.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester, Boston police reported.
The missing teen is described as 5'8", with brown eyes, black hair, and is known to frequent specific Boston areas.
Police urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips via Boston Police CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: An 18-year-old man, Tyler Broomes, was charged in a Dorchester shooting on May 10, injuring two people.
The incident occurred at 69 Kingsdale St., affecting a woman and a man, both aged 42, on Mother's Day.
Broomes is held without bail, facing charges like assault with intent to murder and firearm violations.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Theoretical 2026 lineup of former Red Sox players includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Rafael Devers.
Former Red Sox pitchers Chris Sale, Nathan Eovaldi, and others have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs; Dominic Smith batting over .350 as of May 15, 2026.
